content.text: Why Reconciliation Is Non-Trivial for GenAI

GenAI cross-charges involve three distinct number sets that must agree: the provider invoice (what the platform actually paid), the internal ledger (charges recorded at rate-card prices), and team budgets (what teams expect to pay). Discrepancies between any two of these sets — from missing log entries, stale rate card versions, or provider corrections — generate disputes that erode trust in the FinOps system if not resolved cleanly.

Reconciliation compares the provider invoice to the internal ledger for the same model and period. The ledger records the provider-equivalent cost (before markup) alongside the internal charge (with markup), which enables a clean comparison to the actual invoice.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class ReconciliationStatus(Enum):
    OK        = "ok"          # variance ≤ 2%
    FLAGGED   = "flagged"     # 2–10% — needs investigation
    ESCALATED = "escalated"   # > 10% — hold payment, escalate
    RESOLVED  = "resolved"

@dataclass
class ProviderInvoiceLine:
    model_id: str
    period: str               # YYYY-MM
    input_tokens: int
    output_tokens: int
    billed_usd: float
    invoice_id: str

@dataclass
class InternalLedgerSummary:
    model_id: str
    period: str
    recorded_input_tokens: int
    recorded_output_tokens: int
    internal_charged_usd: float   # at internal rate (with markup)
    provider_cost_usd: float      # what the platform expected to pay externally

@dataclass
class ReconciliationResult:
    period: str
    model_id: str
    provider_billed_usd: float
    internal_provider_cost_usd: float   # recorded in ledger
    variance_usd: float
    variance_pct: float
    status: ReconciliationStatus
    notes: str = ""
    credit_issued_usd: float = 0.0

For each provider invoice line, look up the matching ledger summary. Compute the variance as invoice minus recorded provider cost. Classify the result: OK (≤ 2%), FLAGGED (2–10%), or ESCALATED (> 10%). A missing ledger entry is always ESCALATED regardless of amount — it indicates a logging or attribution failure.

def reconcile_period(
    invoices: list[ProviderInvoiceLine],
    ledger: list[InternalLedgerSummary],
    tolerance_ok: float = 0.02,
    tolerance_flag: float = 0.10,
) -> list[ReconciliationResult]:
    ledger_by_model = {l.model_id: l for l in ledger}
    results = []

    for inv in invoices:
        led = ledger_by_model.get(inv.model_id)
        if led is None:
            results.append(ReconciliationResult(
                period=inv.period,
                model_id=inv.model_id,
                provider_billed_usd=inv.billed_usd,
                internal_provider_cost_usd=0.0,
                variance_usd=inv.billed_usd,
                variance_pct=1.0,
                status=ReconciliationStatus.ESCALATED,
                notes="No matching ledger entry — unrecognised model or missing logging",
            ))
            continue

        variance = inv.billed_usd - led.provider_cost_usd
        pct = abs(variance) / inv.billed_usd if inv.billed_usd else 0.0

        status = (
            ReconciliationStatus.OK        if pct <= tolerance_ok   else
            ReconciliationStatus.FLAGGED   if pct <= tolerance_flag  else
            ReconciliationStatus.ESCALATED
        )

        results.append(ReconciliationResult(
            period=inv.period,
            model_id=inv.model_id,
            provider_billed_usd=inv.billed_usd,
            internal_provider_cost_usd=led.provider_cost_usd,
            variance_usd=variance,
            variance_pct=pct,
            status=status,
            notes=(
                "Within tolerance"         if status == ReconciliationStatus.OK else
                "Token count mismatch — investigate logging gaps" if abs(variance) < 100 else
                "Large discrepancy — check for untagged traffic or rate card version mismatch"
            ),
        ))
    return results

Hold payment to the provider only for ESCALATED variances where you cannot explain the discrepancy. For FLAGGED items, pay the invoice and investigate in parallel — withholding payment while investigating risks late-payment penalties and is not justified for small variances.

Flagged and escalated variances have four common causes. Diagnosing the cause before raising a dispute saves time: provider adjustments and rounding errors require no action; untagged traffic requires a logging fix; rate card mismatches require a recompute and credit.

from enum import Enum

class VarianceCause(Enum):
    UNTAGGED_TRAFFIC   = "untagged_traffic"     # calls not captured in ledger
    RATE_CARD_MISMATCH = "rate_card_mismatch"   # stale rate used at charge time
    PROVIDER_ADJUSTMENT= "provider_adjustment"  # provider-issued credit or correction
    ROUNDING           = "rounding"             # floating-point accumulation < $1
    UNKNOWN            = "unknown"

def diagnose_variance(result: ReconciliationResult) -> VarianceCause:
    """Heuristic root-cause assignment for a flagged reconciliation result.""""
    if abs(result.variance_usd) < 1.0:
        return VarianceCause.ROUNDING

    token_ratio = result.internal_provider_cost_usd / result.provider_billed_usd if result.provider_billed_usd else 0
    if token_ratio < 0.90:
        # We recorded far fewer tokens than provider billed — likely missing logs
        return VarianceCause.UNTAGGED_TRAFFIC

    if result.variance_usd < 0:
        # Provider billed less than we expected — likely a provider credit or discount
        return VarianceCause.PROVIDER_ADJUSTMENT

    # Positive variance where tokens roughly match — rate card version issue
    return VarianceCause.RATE_CARD_MISMATCH
Add missing cost tags; back-fill ledger
Enforce tag gate in API middleware
Recompute at correct version; issue credit

Teams dispute charges when they believe the quantity or rate applied to their account was wrong. Disputes are resolved by recomputing the charge from raw call logs using the rate card version active at the time of each call. A 5-business-day SLA keeps disputes from blocking monthly budget reporting.

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date, timedelta
from enum import Enum

class DisputeStatus(Enum):
    OPEN      = "open"
    UNDER_REVIEW = "under_review"
    UPHELD    = "upheld"     # team's claim is valid — credit issued
    REJECTED  = "rejected"   # platform charge is correct
    WITHDRAWN = "withdrawn"

@dataclass
class DisputeTicket:
    ticket_id: str
    team: str
    period: str
    disputed_amount_usd: float
    reason: str
    evidence_urls: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    opened_date: date = field(default_factory=date.today)
    status: DisputeStatus = DisputeStatus.OPEN
    resolution_note: str = ""
    credit_amount_usd: float = 0.0

    @property
    def sla_deadline(self) -> date:
        return self.opened_date + timedelta(days=5)

    @property
    def is_sla_breached(self) -> bool:
        return date.today() > self.sla_deadline and self.status in (
            DisputeStatus.OPEN, DisputeStatus.UNDER_REVIEW
        )

def resolve_dispute(
    ticket: DisputeTicket,
    call_log_rows: list[dict],
    rate_card_version: int,
) -> DisputeTicket:
    """
    Recompute the charge from raw call logs and compare to what was billed.
    Upholds the dispute if recomputed charge differs by more than 1%.
    """"
    recomputed = sum(
        (row["input_tokens"] / 1_000_000 * row["rate_input_usd"] +
         row["output_tokens"] / 1_000_000 * row["rate_output_usd"])
        for row in call_log_rows
        if row.get("rate_card_version") == rate_card_version
    )
    delta = ticket.disputed_amount_usd - recomputed
    delta_pct = abs(delta) / ticket.disputed_amount_usd if ticket.disputed_amount_usd else 0

    if delta_pct > 0.01:
        ticket.status = DisputeStatus.UPHELD
        ticket.credit_amount_usd = delta
        ticket.resolution_note = (
            f"Recomputed charge at v{rate_card_version}: ${recomputed:.4f}. "
            f"Delta ${delta:.4f} ({delta_pct:.1%}) — credit issued."
        )
    else:
        ticket.status = DisputeStatus.REJECTED
        ticket.resolution_note = (
            f"Recomputed charge at v{rate_card_version}: ${recomputed:.4f}. "
            f"Delta {delta_pct:.2%} — within tolerance. Charge upheld."
        )
    return ticket
: Every LLM call for the team in the disputed period — model, tokens, timestamp, rate card version used.
: The exact rates active at the time of each call. Stored immutably in the rate card store (see Internal Rate Card Design).
: The recomputed total from resolve_dispute() with a line showing the delta and whether it exceeds the 1% tolerance.
: The provider line items for the same model and period, to cross-check that the platform's own cost basis is correct.

After reconciliation completes and disputes are resolved, the finance team posts journal entries to the general ledger. Provider cost is recorded as COGS; internal cross-charges are recorded as inter-department revenue. Credit memos from upheld disputes generate offsetting entries in the same period.

from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date

@dataclass
class GLJournalEntry:
    entry_id: str
    period: str
    debit_account: str      # e.g. "5200-GenAI-COGS"
    credit_account: str     # e.g. "2100-Accrued-Liabilities"
    amount_usd: float
    description: str
    source: str             # "cross_charge" | "credit_memo" | "reconciliation_adj"
    posted_date: date

def build_monthly_journal_entries(
    recon_results: list[ReconciliationResult],
    team_charges: dict[str, float],   # team -> total charged this period
    period: str,
) -> list[GLJournalEntry]:
    entries = []

    # 1. Record provider cost as COGS
    total_provider_cost = sum(r.provider_billed_usd for r in recon_results
                              if r.status != ReconciliationStatus.ESCALATED)
    entries.append(GLJournalEntry(
        entry_id=f"GEN-AI-COGS-{period}",
        period=period,
        debit_account="5200-GenAI-COGS",
        credit_account="2100-Accrued-Liabilities",
        amount_usd=total_provider_cost,
        description=f"GenAI provider cost — {period}",
        source="cross_charge",
        posted_date=date.today(),
    ))

    # 2. Record internal cross-charges as inter-department revenue
    for team, amount in team_charges.items():
        entries.append(GLJournalEntry(
            entry_id=f"XCHG-{team}-{period}",
            period=period,
            debit_account=f"1300-Receivable-{team.upper()}",
            credit_account="4500-Internal-GenAI-Revenue",
            amount_usd=amount,
            description=f"GenAI cross-charge — {team} — {period}",
            source="cross_charge",
            posted_date=date.today(),
        ))

    return entries
Notify teams of charges; open dispute window
: % of invoice lines with variance > 2%. Target < 5%. Rising variance indicates logging gaps or rate card deployment issues.
: % of team charges disputed. Target < 2%. High dispute rate indicates poor communication or opaque billing.
: % of disputes where the platform was wrong. Target < 20%. High uphold rate means billing errors are systemic — fix the root cause.
: % of disputes resolved within 5 business days. Target > 95%. Missed SLAs cause budget reporting delays.

Cross-charge reconciliation compares three number sets monthly: the provider invoice, the internal ledger at provider cost, and the team charges at rate-card prices. Variances are classified by severity, diagnosed by root cause, and resolved through a structured dispute process with a 5-day SLA. Every call is tagged with the rate card version used so disputes can be resolved by exact recomputation rather than estimation. GL journal entries close the loop between the FinOps layer and the finance system, ensuring the accounting records match the technical charge calculations.
